articles.author: Michelle Starr
articles.title: World's Oldest Known 'Octopus' Turns Out to Be An Entirely Different Animal - ScienceAlert
articles.description: A fossil that was fundamental to describing the evolution of octopuses turns out to have been an impostor, all along.
articles.content:
A fossil that was fundamental to describing the evolution of octopuses turns out to have been an impostor, all along.
The famous 300-million-year-old Pohlsepia mazonensis, discovered in 2000 and lat… [+4525 chars]

articles.author: Rachel Treisman
articles.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife - NPR
articles.description: The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in 2020.
articles.content:
The Artemis II astronauts broke the record for the farthest distance from Earth ever traveled by humans on Monday, becoming the first to lay eyes on some parts of the far side of the moon.
